I read the prompt and the prior-investigation log as an external research record. The prompt fixes the special tame-format submission: first generators sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080. The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate, the dynamic tame-inertia word below; earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is immaterial in this word, because that exponent is applied only to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

What I regard as actually established from the prior turns The strongest prior computations are not a proof of the presentation, but they do establish a serious consistency package for the candidate:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4 for the finite-tame pro-2 kernel Ue,f, with nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contributing only unit pivots. The mod-2 cup-product checks, the sign-kernel checks, and the corrected first-IA framing are all candidate-side verifications; they do not identify the arithmetic tame action.The two algebraic conventions that should be frozen are best written as equations, not as informal fractions.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, with t the deck involution and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 a nontrivial odd tame character,Da=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít,soDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

Thus Da is radical, and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is a unit.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

The elimination polynomial is defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.Then h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, because (1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1),and 2s+1 is coprime to f. This is the clean version of the repeated reciprocal issue in the prior log.New contribution this turn: the next first-IA test should be (e,f)=(9,6), not (17,8) or (91,12)

Turn 59‚Äôs anti-cyclotomic reduction is important, but its next-test consequence was not fully extracted. Let f=2n be even and letőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©.

The first mixed marked-IA obstruction only sees d=gcd(e,2n+1),the anti-cyclotomic part of tame inertia. The mixed directions are indexed by the involutionsőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČn,j(1+2n)‚Č°0(mode).

Writing e=de‚ąí, the involution condition forces j=e‚ąíu, u‚ąąZ/dZ, and the fiber from őĒe,f‚ÜíőĒd,f has odd size, so over F2 the mixed direction is pulled back from őĒd,f. Thus the mixed first-IA space is naturallyF2[Cd],up to the two universal square directions b[2]and S[2].Now add the normal-basis gauge. Changing the regular Kummer normal-basis generator by a unit u‚ąąF2[Cd]√ómultiplies this mixed coordinate by a unit, up to the already-noted Frobenius-lift reindexing. Therefore the gauge-invariant content of a nonzero arithmetic-minus-candidate residual is not a named vector Zj, but its support in the Wedderburn decompositionF2[Cd]‚ČÉ Ōē‚ą£Td‚ąí1‚ąŹF2degŌē.

This gives a sharper testing hierarchy:f246812 n=f/212346d=2n+13591765F2[Cd]¬†factor¬†degrees1,21,4 1,2,6 1,8,81,4,12,12,12,12,12 So (5,4) is the full anti-cyclotomic test for f=4, as Turn 59 noted. But the next structurally new first-IA test is (e,f)=(9,6), not (17,8) or (91,12). The reason is that d=9 is the first anti-cyclotomic order whose mixed gauge module has three simple components:F2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.

The smaller quotient (3,6) only sees the C3 -level part; (9,6) also sees the primitive 9-part. Meanwhile (91,12) reduces at first IA to d=13, andF2[C13]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4096,which is larger but not structurally richer at the support-orbit level than (5,4).This does not refute the candidate. It makes the next arithmetic comparison more efficient: after (5,4), compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate residual forL=Q2(ő∂9,ŌĖ),ŌĖ9=2,with a fixed Frobenius lift and fixed normal-basis generator. Report the two square coordinates and the mixed residual‚Äôs three support components inF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.The candidate-normalized target remains zero. Any nonzero support component would be a genuine first-IA obstruction to the live word; zero would move the comparison to the next filtered layer or to a formation theorem.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I froze the two recurring algebraic conventions asDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.Building on the anti-cyclotomic reduction, I identified the mixed first-IA obstruction module as F2[Cd], d=gcd(e,2f/2+1), modulo normal-basis gauge. This shows that the next structurally new first-IA arithmetic test after (5,4) is (9,6), because F2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is an arithmetic comparison or dyadic formation theorem showing that the actual local Galois action has the same filtered affine origins as the candidate over every finite tame subextension. The first-IA difference spaces are now well organized, but their arithmetic residuals have not been computed.

NEXT: Compute the candidate-normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate first-IA residual for (e,f)=(9,6), with fixed Frobenius-lift and normal-basis gauges. Decompose the mixed part in F2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64; the target is zero in all components, plus zero in the two universal square directions.[BREAKTHROUGH]
